pattern: The total cost is a convex function of the chosen target, minimized at the cost-weighted median, so the optimal target is always one of the existing values.
hints: Picking a target value, the cost is the sum over i of cost[i] times the distance from nums[i] to that target.
This cost is convex in the target, so its minimum sits at a value where half the total weight lies on each side.
Sort the values, accumulate weights until you pass half the total cost, and evaluate the cost at that weighted-median value.

edge_inputs.description: one element carries dominant weight pulling the target
uniform costs reduce to plain median
two distinct values with equal weight, target at either is tied

stress.description: moderate array of random values and weights to stress the weighted median
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    cost = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums, cost]
